title: Weight Initialization
description: A ground-up treatment of all PyTorch and TensorFlow/Keras weight initializers — from constant and random baselines to variance-scaling methods (Xavier/Glorot, He/Kaiming, LeCun) and orthogonal initialization. Covers the variance-propagation derivations, default layer behaviors, and a practical selection guide by architecture and activation.

readings.title: Why Initialization Matters
readings.description: The symmetry-breaking problem, variance propagation through layers, and why the wrong starting point causes signals and gradients to explode or collapse.

readings.title: Constant & Identity Initializations
readings.description: zeros, ones, constant, eye, identity, and dirac — what each does, when it is appropriate, and why all-zeros weights break symmetry in practice.

readings.title: Random Distribution Initializations
readings.description: Uniform, normal, truncated normal, and sparse initializations — the role of scale, why naïve N(0,1) fails, and how to pick the right standard deviation.

readings.title: Xavier / Glorot Initialization
readings.description: The variance-propagation derivation, fan-in/fan-out trade-off, uniform vs normal variants, the gain parameter, and when to prefer Xavier over He.

readings.title: He / Kaiming & LeCun Initialization
readings.description: Why ReLU invalidates Xavier, the corrected variance formula for ReLU/LeakyReLU, fan modes, the a parameter, and LeCun initialization for SELU.

readings.title: Orthogonal & Structured Initializations
readings.description: Orthogonal initialization via SVD, why it prevents exploding/vanishing gradients in RNNs, dirac initialization for convolutional layers, and sparse initialization.

readings.title: Practical Guide & Default Behaviors
readings.description: PyTorch and Keras layer defaults, how to override initialization, the gain table, and a decision guide for every activation-architecture combination.

labs.title: Weight Initialization in PyTorch
labs.description: Apply every torch.nn.init function, run variance-propagation experiments, compare convergence across initializers on a deep MLP, and verify orthogonality numerically.

labs.title: Weight Initialization in TensorFlow
labs.description: Use all tf.keras.initializers, verify parity with PyTorch equivalents, and compare training stability of Xavier vs He on CIFAR-10 with different activations.
